unit.name: Contrastive learning formulation and InfoNCE
unit.description: Shifts from task-specific transformations to a general attract-repel objective based on positive and negative pairs, then derives the intuition behind InfoNCE.
unit.summary: The lecture transitions from hand-designed pretext tasks to a more general idea: transformed views of the same object should be close in representation space, while views from different objects should be pushed apart [ts=3590s] [ts=3634s]. It formalizes contrastive learning with a reference sample x, positive samples x+ derived from the same object, and negative samples x- from other objects, then asks the score of the positive pair to exceed the scores of negatives [ts=3638s] [ts=3675s]. This leads to a softmax-style loss over one positive and many negatives that the lecturer explicitly identifies as the same core idea as multiclass cross-entropy, now used for contrastive representation learning [ts=3709s] [ts=3837s]. The segment names this objective InfoNCE and states two theoretical takeaways: minimizing InfoNCE maximizes a lower bound on mutual information between positive pairs, and using more negative samples tightens that bound, which explains the need for large batches in many contrastive methods [ts=3843s] [ts=3947s].

key_points.text: Contrastive learning treats transformed views of the same object as positives and other objects as negatives.
The loss encourages score(x, x+) to be larger than scores between the reference and negative samples.
The contrastive objective can be interpreted as an N-way softmax / multiclass cross-entropy problem over one positive and many negatives.
InfoNCE is described as a lower bound on mutual information between positive pairs.
More negative samples tighten the bound, which is why large batch sizes help contrastive training.

kp_catalog.name: Contrastive learning and InfoNCE
kp_catalog.description: A general SSL formulation that attracts positive pairs and repels negatives, optimized with the InfoNCE objective.
kp_catalog.importance_rationale: Important because contrastive learning and InfoNCE formalizes how a general SSL formulation that attracts positive pairs and repels negatives, optimized with the InfoNCE objective, which later units use as core machinery rather than background context.
